fields.default: Remove lock out and tag out. Ensure no tools are left within the DB Panel. The main feeder is switched ON and the MCB line resistance parameter is noted using Multi-meter.

blocks.subtitle: Following Tests were carried Out
items: 1. Physical Test.
2. IR Test.

blocks.subtitle: Physical Test
items: 1. Equipment Position & Condition.
2. Panel flanges & Chamber Cover.
3. Door Locks & Gasket.
4. Panel coupling and fish plate.
5. Busbar Connection & its supports.
6. Earthing Connection & Continuity.
7. Nuts/bolts/screws/fasteners are Tight.
8. Incoming & Outgoing Cable Connection.
9. Power & Control Wiring Connections.
10. Indication Lamp, Push Button, Selector Switch.
11. Electrical / Mechanical Interlock Check.
12. Meter & CT / PT Connection.
13. Relay / Protective Devices.
14. On/Off Operation of Breaker.

blocks.subtitle: IR Test
items: 1. Switch the power supply breaker to the "Open" or "Off" position.
2. De-energize load side connections.
3. Isolate all loads associated with the bus to be tested.
4. Disconnect high-voltage sensitive devices and potential transformers from buses to be tested.

5. Megger bus work phase-to-phase then phase-to-ground for one minute, until readings are stable.
    a. Phase To Phase R – Y , Y – B , B – R
    b. Phase To Neutral R – N , Y – N , B – N
    c. Phase To Earth R – E , Y – E , B – E
    d. Neutral To Earth N – E
6. Ground and discharge each phase tested.

items.particular: Indication Lamp, Push Button, Selector Switch.

blocks.subtitle: Test Procedure
items: 1. Inspect the system to verify that openings are plugged and valves are closed.
2. Connect the test pump to a convenient location in the system.
3. Connect the water source to the test pump.
4. Open the main control valve to fill the system. (If the water service is not installed or has not been flushed, fill the system through the test pump water source.)
5. Fill the system slowly to avoid entrapment of air.
6. Open a valve (such as the inspector's test connection or a temporary valve) to vent any trapped air.
7. Close the valve when water flows continuously through it.

8. When the system pressure equals that of the water source, close the supply valve and inspect the entire system for leaks.
   i. Leaks may result from flanges not bolted properly, plugs not properly installed, cracked or improperly tightened fittings, etc.
   ii. If leaks are found, open the 2-in. main drain connection or other low-point drain connection and allow the system to drain. Repair any leaks found and repeat this procedure.
   iii. If no leaks are detected, begin to increase pressure with the test pump up to 200 psi or 50 psi in excess of the static pressure when static pressure exceeds 150 psi.
9. Monitor the test gauge to determine that the system pressure is stable. If pressure drops, check for leaks in the system, for open valves, or for leaks in the test apparatus.

10. Once it is determined that the test pressure has stabilized, disconnect the power to the test pump and notify the commissioning agent or authority having jurisdiction that the 2-hour test period has begun.
11. Record the time of day and test pressure at this time.
12. Hold the test pressure for 2 hours.
13. After the test, open the drain valve to drain the system, or if the system is to be commissioned immediately, relieve the test pressure and leave the water in the system.
14. Disconnect the test pump and plug the outlet or test port through which the system was tested.
15. If the test will be required on existing fire sprinkler systems, review the proper impairment including the fire alarm system to avoid false signals.

blocks.subtitle: Measurements
items: 1. Test pressure is to be maintained for 2 hours without any visible leaks.

blocks.subtitle: Pass/Fail Criteria
items: 1. The system must hold the test pressure for 2 hours without loss of pressure.
2. Absence of water leakage is verified by visual examination of the system.
